TMC.title: Why Is TMC Stock Gaining Monday?

macro_events.event_summary: Saudi Aramco's CEO warns the oil market won't return to normal until 2027 if Strait of Hormuz disruption persists, implying a prolonged supply shock and elevated crude prices.
primary_drivers: Sustained crude price elevation from constrained Gulf supply transit
Tanker rates surge from rerouting and war-risk premiums
Margin compression for crude-consuming industries (refiners on differentials, airlines on jet fuel)

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated with significant upstream leverage to Brent; non-Gulf production (Permian, Guyana) is unaffected by Hormuz, so realized prices rise without volume loss. Every $10/bbl sustained move adds ~$4-5B to annual upstream earnings.
Similar to XOM — Permian and Kazakhstan/Tengiz upstream benefit from higher Brent without Hormuz transit risk on US barrels. Strong free cash flow leverage to sustained $90+ crude.
Pure-play Permian E&P with no international exposure; ~100% of production sells into a market priced off Brent-linked benchmarks. Highest beta to crude among large US independents.
US onshore-heavy producer with high operating leverage and elevated debt; sustained $90+ crude through 2027 dramatically accelerates deleveraging and buyback capacity.

Frontline is the largest pure-play crude tanker operator; Hormuz disruption forces rerouting around Africa, doubling ton-miles and spiking VLCC day-rates. War-risk premiums flow directly to spot-exposed fleets.
Scorpio Tankers operates product tankers; refined product flows from Gulf refineries get rerouted, driving MR/LR rates higher. Direct beneficiary of Hormuz transit risk premia.
Targa's Permian gathering/processing and LPG export franchise benefits from US becoming the marginal global supplier as Gulf flows are impaired; export economics improve materially.
Cheniere is the largest US LNG exporter; Hormuz disruption threatens Qatari LNG (~20% of global supply) transit, structurally re-rating US LNG netbacks and contract pricing power.

losers.rationale: Delta has no fuel hedges and the highest jet fuel exposure as % of opex among US legacies; sustained crude through 2027 directly compresses margins by billions. Most fuel-sensitive name.
United similarly unhedged on fuel with large international long-haul exposure (higher fuel burn per ASM). Sustained $90+ crude erodes the post-pandemic margin recovery thesis.
Southwest hedges have largely rolled off; point-to-point domestic model means fuel is ~30%+ of opex with limited pricing power to pass through at the low-fare end of the market.
Dollar Tree's fixed-price/low-price model has minimal ability to pass through diesel-driven freight cost increases; low-income consumer also gets squeezed by gasoline prices, hitting traffic.
Carnival's bunker fuel costs spike with crude; cruise demand is also discretionary and sensitive to consumer fuel-price-driven cost-of-living pressure. Double hit on cost and demand.

Dow's ethylene/petrochemical business sees naphtha and ethane feedstock costs rise with crude; global competitors using stranded Gulf gas lose access, but Dow's margins compress before pricing catches up.
Paccar truck OEM faces demand destruction as fleet operators delay capex amid diesel cost spike and freight-rate uncertainty; Class 8 orders historically roll over on sustained fuel shocks.
Marriott's leisure transient demand correlates inversely with gasoline prices; sustained crude through 2027 dampens US road-trip travel and international airfare-sensitive bookings.

headlines: Saudi Aramco CEO says oil market won't normalize until 2027 if Hormuz disruption persists

macro_events.event_summary: Trump and congressional Republicans are floating suspending the federal gasoline tax (18.4¢/gal) to cushion consumers from rising pump prices amid the Iran war.
primary_drivers: Effective retail gasoline price cut at the pump increases gasoline demand elasticity and protects refiner/retailer margins
Consumer discretionary income relief at the margin
Signals war-driven crude prices remain elevated (bullish backdrop for upstream energy persists)

beneficiaries.rationale: Marathon Petroleum is the largest US refiner with ~2,900 Speedway-legacy retail outlets; a federal gas tax holiday lowers pump prices without compressing refiner crack spreads, supporting volumes through its retail/midstream chain.
Phillips 66 — large US refining footprint plus ~7,000 branded retail stations; tax holiday boosts gasoline demand elasticity and supports refining throughput utilization without margin hit.
Valero is a pure-play US refiner with heavy Gulf Coast gasoline output; lower pump prices via tax suspension support demand at a time when wartime crude costs threaten demand destruction.
Casey's General Stores — fuel retailer where lower pump prices typically drive higher gallons sold and higher in-store traffic/merchandise attach; direct beneficiary of a federal gas tax suspension.
Murphy USA — low-cost fuel retailer whose volume/traffic model is highly sensitive to retail gasoline price; tax holiday boosts gallons sold and convenience-store basket.
Walmart sells discounted fuel at Sam's/Walmart stations and is the largest exposure to lower-income consumers whose discretionary spend is most squeezed by pump prices; small but real macro tailwind.
Cruise lines and discretionary travel companies benefit from any consumer fuel-cost relief that preserves vacation budgets during wartime price spike; sentiment trade on consumer wallet.

losers.rationale: Vulcan Materials — the federal gas tax funds the Highway Trust Fund which pays for road construction aggregate demand; suspending it (even temporarily) raises questions about HTF solvency and FY infrastructure spend pacing.
Martin Marietta — same mechanism as VMC: aggregates demand is directly tied to federally funded highway projects financed by the gas tax; a suspension threatens the funding mechanism.

Granite Construction is a pure-play highway/road contractor; federal gas tax revenue is the primary funding source for the Highway Trust Fund and any suspension creates overhang on project funding visibility.
Cheaper gasoline at the pump (via tax cut on top of any war premium relief) marginally weakens the TCO case for EVs versus ICE vehicles; small headwind to EV demand narrative.

headlines: Trump, congressional Republicans float suspending federal gas tax amid Iran war

macro_events.event_summary: MarketWatch flags accelerating inflation with concern over how high it could go, implying upside risk to CPI prints and a more hawkish Fed path.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer rates / rising real yields pressure long-duration assets
Pricing power and real-asset exposure benefit from sustained inflation pass-through
Margin compression for low-pricing-power consumer-facing names as input costs rise faster than they can pass on

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated oil major with direct revenue leverage to higher commodity prices, a primary inflation transmission channel; energy is one of the few sectors whose earnings scale with CPI components.
Same mechanism as XOM — upstream production revenue rises with crude/gas; historically positive correlation with CPI surprises.
Largest US-listed gold miner; gold tends to bid on persistent inflation narratives and dollar-debasement fears, and Newmont has high operating leverage to spot gold.
Demonstrated pricing power and trade-down beneficiary; in prior inflation cycles (2022) Walmart gained share from higher-income consumers trading down for groceries.

losers.rationale: Dollar General serves low-income consumers least able to absorb inflation; prior CPI spikes have compressed both traffic and basket size, and DG has limited pricing power vs. WMT.
Cruise operator with massive fixed-rate and floating-rate debt load; higher-for-longer rates raise refinancing costs while discretionary travel demand softens under inflation strain.
Realty Income — long-duration net-lease REIT whose dividend is benchmarked against the 10Y; rising real yields compress the multiple and raise its cost of capital for acquisitions.

headlines: Inflation is getting worse. Just how bad is it going to get?

macro_events.event_summary: IREN (Iris Energy) shares pull back as investors digest the high capex required for its AI/HPC data center buildout pivot from Bitcoin mining.
primary_drivers: Capex sticker shock on AI infrastructure buildout costs
Investor scrutiny of crypto-miner-to-AI-host pivot economics
Re-rating of speculative AI infrastructure names

beneficiaries.rationale: Confirmation that AI hosts like IREN are spending heavily on GPU buildout reinforces continued demand for H100/H200/Blackwell GPUs — IREN's capex IS NVDA revenue.
Vertiv supplies power and cooling infrastructure to AI data centers; heavy capex spend by AI hosts like IREN directly translates to orders for liquid cooling and power distribution.

losers.rationale: Direct subject of the story — stock pulling back on capex concerns around its AI/HPC pivot.
Cipher Mining is a peer Bitcoin miner pivoting to HPC/AI hosting; same capex-burden narrative applies and peer rotation likely.
TeraWulf is pursuing the same miner-to-AI-host transition with major buildout costs; correlated trade with IREN on capex skepticism.
Core Scientific is the most prominent Bitcoin-miner-turned-AI-host (CoreWeave contract); same investor skepticism on AI buildout economics applies.
Applied Digital is a pure-play AI/HPC data center buildout story carrying heavy capex with uncertain returns — direct read-through.
Hut 8 is another miner with AI/HPC ambitions; rotation lower with the cohort on capex concerns.

headlines: Iren’s stock pulls back as investors assess the steep price of the AI buildout
